sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current bar: 41 P + 23 c, transition at col 41
# Let me try clicking on the charcoal section (col 63, the rightmost)
# and see if it removes pixels

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Before: {ascii_lines[0]}")

# Click on the rightmost charcoal pixel
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 0, 'col': 63}])

ascii_lines2 = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"After clicking (0,63): {ascii_lines2[0]}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('P', 'c'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']} pixels")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")

# Also let me check if clicking on the BAR ITSELF (id=1, the charcoal section) does something
# Let me also try clicking on the transition point (col 41)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 0, 'col': 41}])

ascii_lines3 = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nAfter clicking (0,41): {ascii_lines3[0]}")
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('P', 'c'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']} pixels")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
